question_text: A free-body diagram shows four forces acting on a stationary box: a downward arrow labelled 50 N (weight), an upward arrow labelled 50 N (normal force), a rightward arrow labelled 20 N (push), and a leftward arrow labelled 20 N (friction). What is the resultant force on the box?
explanation: The resultant force is found by adding all forces together, considering their directions. Vertical forces: 50 N down − 50 N up = 0 N. Horizontal forces: 20 N right − 20 N left = 0 N. Total resultant = 0 N, which means the box is in equilibrium and will not accelerate.
